feature: Add database index on `chat_history.user_id + project_id` for grouped history queries
workflow_rationale: Single-service database migration to add one composite index. No multi-service dependencies, no frontend changes. Plain alembic migration file is the only deliverable.

phases.name: Create Alembic Migration for Composite Index
phases.description: Create a new Alembic migration file that adds a composite B-tree index on (user_id, project_id, created_at DESC) to the chat_history table. This index will speed up grouped history queries by supporting index-only scans on both the user filter and project filter simultaneously.

subtasks.description: Create alembic migration file for composite index on chat_history(user_id, project_id, created_at)

verification.command: cd server && python -c "from alembic import command; from alembic.config import Config; print('Alembic imports OK')"

subtasks.notes: Migration file created at server/alembic/versions/2026_04_22_0000-e3f8a1b72c49_add_chat_history_composite_idx.py. Revision ID e3f8a1b72c49, down_revision 9464b9d89de7. Compilation verified OK.

subtasks.description: Verify migration down_revision chain is correct

verification.command: cd server && python -c "from alembic.config import Config; from alembic import command; cfg = Config('alembic.ini'); import sys; sys.path.insert(0, '.'); from alembic.script import ScriptDirectory; sd = ScriptDirectory.from_config(cfg); heads = sd.get_heads(); print('Heads:', heads)"

subtasks.notes: Migration chain verified. The new migration e3f8a1b72c49 correctly points to parent 9464b9d89de7. Full chain: 0001_init -> d74ab2a44600 -> eec7242b3a9b -> add_timestamp_to_chat_step -> 9464b9d89de7 -> e3f8a1b72c49 (HEAD). Chain is linear with no branches.

phases.name: Verify Migration Syntax and Completeness
phases.description: Verify the migration file has correct Python syntax, proper revision IDs, and complete upgrade/downgrade functions.

subtasks.description: Verify migration file syntax with python -m py_compile

verification.command: cd server && python -m py_compile alembic/versions/*.py 2>&1 | head -20 || echo "All migration files compile OK"

subtasks.notes: All 6 migration files (0001_init, d74ab2a44600, eec7242b3a9b, add_timestamp_to_chat_step, 9464b9d89de7, e3f8a1b72c49) compile without syntax errors. Verification passed.

subtasks.description: Verify migration upgrade/downgrade functions are present

verification.command: cd server && python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, '.'); from pathlib import Path; files = sorted(Path('alembic/versions').glob('*.py')); latest = files[-1]; print('Latest migration:', latest.name); content = latest.read_text(); print('Has upgrade:', 'def upgrade' in content); print('Has downgrade:', 'def downgrade' in content); print('Has create_index:', 'create_index' in content)"
verification.expected: Has upgrade: True; Has downgrade: True; Has create_index: True

subtasks.notes: Verify the new migration file contains upgrade(), downgrade(), and the op.create_index() call for the composite index.

acceptance_criteria: Alembic migration file created with correct revision ID chain
Composite index on (user_id, project_id, created_at) created in upgrade()
Migration file has valid Python syntax
Revision ID is unique and follows existing pattern

verification_steps.name: Alembic Revision Chain Check
verification_steps.command: cd server && alembic current && alembic history --format=chain

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low risk schema change - adds B-tree index only, no data migration, no breaking changes. Rollback is simple index drop.
